Incident Report — ADGM context — Data Security and MNPI Exposure — 5 June 2026
Reporting entity: Al Nasma Data Holdings PJSC, ADGM Reg. No. ADGM-CL-34219; Abu Dhabi DED Trade Licence CN-7654321; TRN 100234567890123.
Registered office: Unit 1804, Al Samaa Tower, Al Maryah Island, Abu Dhabi, UAE.
Primary contact: Ms. Leila Barqawi, Compliance Officer; tel +971 58 123 4578; email leila.barqawi@alnasmahd.example.
On 28 May 2026, we detected unauthorized SFTP access that exfiltrated a folder titled “ADGM-listing-draft” containing non-public revenue guidance and a term sheet for a proposed private placement; this constitutes MNPI under SCA/ADGM rules.
Files also included PII under UAE PDPL: Emirates ID 784-1979-1234567-1 for Mr. Omar Haddad (Finance), corporate bank IBAN AE12 123 4567890123456789, and passport No. XN0093318 for Ms. Farah Mansoor (external counsel).
Supplier records compromised: Marjan Cyber Services LLC, Dubai DED Licence 9876543, bank IBAN AE55 741 0123456789012345, accounts payable email ap@marjancyber.example.
One document included a scanned family book extract noting Ms. Leila Barqawi’s family origin as “Al Haurani, coastal Sharqi origin,” which is sensitive personal data.
We notified the ADGM Office of Data Protection and the SCA Market Abuse Unit within 72 hours; internal incident ref ADS-IR-2026-044; no regulator case number issued yet.
Placeholder items present in drafts should be ignored as non-data: “Emirates ID: __________”, “TRN: __________”, and “Passport No: ________”.
Public contact channels are not part of the breach scope: customer helpline 800 0000 and info@example.com are generic.
For impacted stakeholders, communications were sent from privacy@alnasmahd.example and call-back number +971 4 555 0420; a separate ADGM counsel line is listed as +971 600 123 000 (service line only).
Remediation steps included SFTP key rotation, forced resets on the ADGM Smart Form accounts, and suspension of ops-inbox@alnasmahd.example.
While core systems remain intact, a confidential “Q3 2026 earnings range” slide and a draft board resolution regarding an ADX dual-listing exploration were accessed; neither item has been publicly disclosed.
